headline: Martine Barrat, a photographer without fear or limits
subhead: At 93, the artist has lost none of her vitality or curiosity. She lives at the legendary Hotel Chelsea in New York, where she remains one of the last year-round artist residents. Today, her work is the subject of a retrospective at the Rencontres d'Arles photography festival.

headline: US launches new strikes on Iran after container ship hit in Hormuz
Why heatwaves disrupt our sleep – and how we can protect it
Police arrest 28-year-old suspect over killing of UK politician
subhead: Former Conservative MP Ann Widdecombe, 78, was found dead with 'serious injuries' on Thursday at her home in Devon, southwest England, according to police. The suspect was arrested in South Yorkshire on Saturday. Another man, aged 26, was detained on Friday but has since been released from custody and dismissed from the investigation.

headline: 'No region is safe from climate change: Brittany will not be a climate refuge'
subhead: Confusing a region's geographic exposure with its vulnerability to climate change can create a false sense of security and increase the unpreparedness of local authorities and residents, warn analysts Clément Jeanneau and Antoine Poincaré.

headline: How live screenings of Paris Fashion Week are making fashion more accessible
subhead: Organized by influencer Lyas, free live screenings of fashion shows on big screens toured France during Paris Men's Fashion Week. Several hundred people gathered in Marseille for the opening night.

headline: Before Emile Zola, this forgotten writer was the first to defend Alfred Dreyfus
subhead: The journalist Bernard Lazare fought relentlessly to defend Captain Dreyfus and denounce the antisemitism that drove his conviction. On Sunday, France will mark its first national day commemorating the rehabilitation of the Jewish officer.

Silicon Valley's next generation feels the AI blues
subhead: In California, the rise of artificial intelligence no longer generates the unanimous enthusiasm it once did. Anxiety is mounting, especially among students and engineers, as jobs have been cut and democratic risks have grown more threatening.

headline: Strait of Hormuz deadlock could lead Trump into sporadic war with Iran
subhead: The renewed US and Iranian strikes on Wednesday, despite the memorandum of understanding reached between the two countries, jeopardize hopes for a swift end to the crisis unpopular with the American public.

headline: After a year eyeing France's presidency, Bardella has to settle for deputy
subhead: On Marine Le Pen's first campaign trip after being cleared to run for president despite her conviction, her would-be stand-in Jordan Bardella looked stern at her side.
